defs: Kind regard; propitious aspect; countenance; friendly disposition; kindness; good will.
quotes: Hath crawled into the favor of the king.

defs: The act of countenancing, or the condition of being countenanced, or regarded propitiously; support; promotion; befriending.
quotes: But found no favor in his lady's eyes.
And Jesus increased in wisdom and stature, and in favor with God and man.

defs: A kind act or office; kindness done or granted; benevolence shown by word or deed; an act of grace or good will, as distinct from justice or remuneration.
quotes: Beg one favor at thy gracious hand.

defs: Mildness or mitigation of punishment; lenity.
quotes: I could not discover the lenity and favor of this sentence.

defs: The object of regard; person or thing favored.
quotes: All these his wondrous works, but chiefly man, His chief delight and favor.

defs: A gift or present; something bestowed as an evidence of good will; a token of love; a knot of ribbons; something worn as a token of affection; as, a marriage favor is a bunch or knot of white ribbons or white flowers worn at a wedding.
quotes: Wear thou this favor for me, and stick it in thy cap.
This boy is fair, of female favor.

defs: A letter or epistle; -- so called in civility or compliment; as, your favor of yesterday is received.

quotes: But, with your favor, I will treat it here.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Favored (fā"vrd); p. pr. & vb. n. Favoring.]

defs: To regard with kindness; to support; to aid, or to have the disposition to aid, or to wish success to; to be propitious to; to treat with consideration or tenderness; to show partiality or unfair bias towards.
quotes: O happy youth! and favored of the skies.
He that favoreth Joab, . . . let him go after Joab.
[The painter] has favored her squint admirably.

defs: To afford advantages for success to; to facilitate; as, a weak place favored the entrance of the enemy.
To resemble in features; to have the aspect or looks of; as, the child favors his father.
quotes: The porter owned that the gentleman favored his master.
